text: Ever wonder why pollen dances in water? It's not magic—it's atoms! This jittery movement is called Brownian motion, named after botanist Robert Brown. Brown noticed that pollen grains zigzagged due to tiny, invisible water molecules colliding with them. This was some of the first evidence for atoms, changing how we understand matter! Brownian motion proves that even the smallest particles can have a big impact! Curious about more scientific wonders? Like and subscribe for more!
